title: VoiceChatModerator
description: A Mod to moderate a Server with SimpleVoiceChat. Includes Speaking Overlay, VoiceChatRecordings, live transcripts and more
body:
# VoiceChatModerator

**VoiceChatModerator** is a client-side Fabric mod for **Simple Voice Chat** that shows you **who is currently speaking**, can optionally display **spoken text**, and archives conversations **locally as audio and text**.

The mod is designed for server moderation, event support, documentation, and anyone who wants to review or search voice chat sessions later.

## Highlights

- **Live speaker overlay** in the HUD
- **Transcript above player name tags**
- **Local recordings** as audio and text
- **In-game browser** for saved sessions
- **Export feature** for individual recordings
- **Optional speech-to-text**
- **Client-side** – no server-side mod required

## What the mod does

While you are using **Simple Voice Chat**, the mod detects incoming voice activity and can make it visible in several ways:

### Speaker Overlay
A clean HUD overlay shows which players are currently speaking. Depending on your configuration, it can display things like:

- player names
- distance
- volume
- short transcript snippets

### Name Tag Transcript
If speech-to-text is enabled, spoken text can be displayed **above the player's head**.

### Recording & Archive
Conversations are saved locally, split by hour and organized by:

- server
- player
- date
- hour

Depending on your settings, this creates:

- **`.wav`** or **`.mp3`** files
- matching **`.txt`** files with transcript content

### Browser & Export
The built-in browser lets you search through your recordings directly in-game and export them when needed.

## Who is this mod for?

VoiceChatModerator is especially useful for:

- **moderation teams** on community servers
- **event or roleplay organization**
- **documenting conversations**
- **reviewing incidents**
- players who want a **better overview of voice chat activity**

## Speech-to-Text

Speech-to-text is **optional** and can be turned off completely.

At the moment, the mod mainly supports these approaches:

- **DIRECT** – directly local inside the mod
- **API** – through an external compatible service

If you only want the overlay and recordings, you can simply leave speech-to-text disabled.

## Hotkeys

- **F6** – toggle overlay
- **F7** – open recording browser
- **F8** – toggle recording

## Requirements

- **Minecraft 1.21.11**
- **Fabric Loader**
- **Fabric API**
- **Simple Voice Chat**
- **Java 21+**
- **ModMenu** optional, but recommended

## Installation

1. Install **Fabric Loader**
2. Put **Fabric API** into your `mods` folder
3. Put **Simple Voice Chat** into your `mods` folder
4. Put **VoiceChatModerator** into your `mods` folder
5. Optionally install **ModMenu** for easier configuration

## Quick Start

### Without Speech-to-Text

If you only want the moderation and recording features at first:

1. Start the game
2. Disable **STT** in the settings
3. Enable recording
4. Use **F7** later to browse saved recordings

### With Speech-to-Text

Enable STT in the configuration and choose a supported method. Spoken content can then appear in the overlay, as name tag text, and in saved transcript files depending on your settings.

## Recording Location

Recordings are stored locally in your Minecraft directory:

```text
.minecraft/VoiceChatRecord/
```

## Important Notes

- This mod is **client-side**.
- It does **not replace Simple Voice Chat**; it extends it.
- Audio and transcripts are stored **locally on your computer**.
- Speech-to-text quality may vary depending on language, audio quality, and background noise.

## In Short

If you want to see **who is speaking**, **what was said**, and keep a **local record of voice conversations** in Minecraft, VoiceChatModerator is made for exactly that.
